summary: The central gate is the right approach and the focused tests pass, but the implementation no longer matches the PR's stated 1-second analytics debounce window. Suppressed providers also still emit the old error-view Sentry breadcrumb, so the Sentry-noise part is only partially addressed.

comments.body: The PR body says rapid error flaps are debounced with a 1-second window, but this constant is 150 ms. That means an error that clears after 200 ms and reappears before 1 second can still emit two `PERPS_SCREEN_VIEWED` events, which violates the stated behavior. Please either restore the 1-second window or update the PR/tests/acceptance criteria if 150 ms is the intended product behavior.

Because `PerpsConnectionProvider` still records its `PerpsConnectionErrorView shown` Sentry breadcrumb whenever `connectionState.error` changes, passing `suppressErrorView` here suppresses the UI but not the provider breadcrumb. With the screen, modal, and close-position providers mounted, that can still duplicate and mislabel Sentry breadcrumbs even though the global gate owns rendering. Consider moving that breadcrumb to `PerpsGlobalErrorGate` or guarding it with `!suppressErrorView`.
